mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is an AI Gateway that allows you to call over 100 LLM APIs in a unified format, providing features like cost tracking, load balancing, and logging. It is ideal for developers looking to integrate multiple LLM services seamlessly. Key trade-offs include the complexity of managing multiple API keys and potential costs associated with API usage.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LiteLLM CLI tool, which is necessary for managing the LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

pip install litellm==1.82.0.patch3

```

> Install the specific version of LiteLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running properly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy LiteLLM
steps.description:
This command deploys the LiteLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.0.patch3/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

```

> Ensure the deployment YAML is compatible with your cluster.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.0.patch3/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Adjust min/max based on expected load.
> Ensure monitoring is set up correctly.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.0.patch3/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will delete the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade LiteLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n litellm -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, status: .status.phase}'

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.0.patch3/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

kubectl rollout status deployment/litellm

```

> Check the status of your pods before proceeding.
> This will apply the latest deployment configuration.
> Verify that the deployment has completed successfully.
commands: kubectl get pods -n litellm -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, status: .status.phase}'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is private.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the resources allocated to existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LiteLLM service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the deployment status and logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** API keys or permissions are not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify that your API keys are correctly configured and have the necessary permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLMs and improved logging features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods' showing the LiteLLM pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
